checks.classification: the original seven-path producer patch remains material-whitespace clean in the index

adversarialRegressionMatrix.case: identity file grows beyond its cap after descriptor stat and before descriptor read
adversarialRegressionMatrix.expected: consume exactly maxBytes + 1, reject before decoding and preserve the parent binding

adversarialRegressionMatrix.case: attempt ownership file grows beyond its cap after descriptor stat and before descriptor read
adversarialRegressionMatrix.expected: consume exactly maxBytes + 1, reject before decoding and retain the artifact

adversarialRegressionMatrix.case: validated attempt leaf is replaced at the atomic quarantine rename boundary
adversarialRegressionMatrix.expected: moved inode mismatch blocks logical success; the original logical path disappears while both the validated object and quarantined replacement sentinel remain physically recoverable

adversarialRegressionMatrix.case: validated team leaf is replaced at the atomic quarantine rename boundary
adversarialRegressionMatrix.expected: moved inode mismatch blocks logical success after durable tombstone; the original logical path disappears while both the validated object and quarantined replacement sentinel remain physically recoverable

adversarialRegressionMatrix.case: successful artifact cleanup and team deletion through nested quarantine containers
adversarialRegressionMatrix.expected: product-visible logical paths are absent, one config-free hidden container remains under each bound parent, and retained content can be recovered by an out-of-lane hosted operation

adversarialRegressionMatrix.case: real TeamConfigReader nonrecursive listing after permanent delete
adversarialRegressionMatrix.expected: the original team is visible before deletion but neither the config-free container nor its nested config-bearing recoverable object is listed after deletion

adversarialRegressionMatrix.case: attempt artifact pristine check encounters reserved quarantine name
adversarialRegressionMatrix.expected: ignore only a no-follow descriptor-proved nested directory container; reject a same-name non-directory impostor before ownership publication

caller forges exact on-disk ownership bytes without durable registry
identity-read ancestor replaced after descriptor acquisition
identity-publication ancestor replaced at final-component open
adversarialRegressionMatrix.expected: blocked with no identity written outside

adversarialRegressionMatrix.expected: blocked before quarantine with outside sentinel unchanged

rootNegativeMatrix.result: descriptor-bound effect or fail-closed binding mismatch; no outside effect

privatePathScan.classification: negative-control labels only; no path value

flatQuarantinePrefixScan.classification: no former direct-child quarantine-prefix naming remains

productionPhysicalDeletionCallScan.classification: no rm, unlink or rmdir call in the four P2.D production paths

selfReview.cleanArchitecture: core defines value-only identity and attempt-registry ports; Node filesystem mechanics remain in main infrastructure; later serial composition selects P2.B-backed implementations
selfReview.ddd: TeamId, LegacyTeamKey, prepared/file_published/committed authority, write-once publication evidence, attempt ownership and durable tombstones are explicit domain language
selfReview.solid: identity registry, attempt ownership registry, identity publication, directory lifecycle and backup compatibility expose separate narrow responsibilities
selfReview.filesystemSecurity: directory descriptors bind effects to admitted directories; no-follow capped reads consume at most maxBytes + 1 and reject size or metadata drift before decoding; request-path deletion ends after atomic rename into one descriptor-proved parent/.p2-d-removal-quarantine/<uuid> container, moved-inode reproof and container/parent fsync, with no unlink, rmdir or recursive rm
selfReview.cleanupCorrectness: logical attempt removal requires matching durable registry state plus canonical write-once provenance, repeats both checks, atomically nests the validated leaf, fails closed unless the moved inode still matches, ignores only a descriptor-proved nested quarantine container during pristine ownership checks, and keeps all quarantined bytes recoverable
selfReview.listingInvisibility: real TeamConfigReader nonrecursive semantics see the team before delete and no team after delete; teamsRoot retains only a config-free hidden container while the config-bearing object stays one level deeper and recoverable
selfReview.sameUidRaceBoundary: pure Node/POSIX check-then-unlink cannot secure a quarantined final name against a same-UID swap; physical retention and GC remain a separate hosted-operations concern outside this product lane
selfReview.recoveryCorrectness: commit consumes only a freshly reread durable file_published record; missing or mismatched publication evidence blocks and committed absence never republishes automatically
selfReview.transportBoundary: no Electron, IPC, HTTP, Fastify, renderer or preload dependency was introduced
selfReview.identityImmutability: exclusive create with mode 0600 only; no overwrite, rename replacement or replacement TeamId path exists
selfReview.dependencyDirection: P2.D depends on its own application ports and the integrated identifier kernel, never on P2.B or P2.C implementation source

resolvedReviewFindings.proof: descriptor-bound no-follow capped reads plus deterministic identity and ownership concurrent-growth regressions
quarantine-only logical deletion with moved-inode reproof, parent fsync, no physical request-path removal, and deterministic artifact/team sentinel recovery regressions

single nested quarantine container per bound parent with descriptor/container/moved-inode reproof, container and parent fsync, real nonrecursive listing invisibility, recoverability, and no physical request-path removal

unverifiedClaims: existing create, delete, provisioning cleanup and backup call-site routing; those paths remain reserved to serial integration
the concrete P2.B and P2.C adapters and production composition
full backup recovery or a coordinated recovery point
the full ADR-28 openat2 hosted guard and final-runtime active-race topology
physical quarantine retention policy and garbage collection, which are hosted-operations concerns outside P2.D
production Electron, transport, provider and runtime behavior
combined architecture/security acceptance, serial integration, milestone acceptance, commit and push

estimateBuckets.classification: bounded P2.D remediation within the existing identity/workspace envelope

nextAction: controller review of this bounded seven-path remediation only; HOLD with no successor, integration, commit or push initiated
